proved: Heuristics have a schema-versioned bounded domain record with stable ID, title, guidance, priority, enabled state, creator identity/time, and attributed edits.
Each heuristic and its edit history use nested Yjs maps rather than opaque last-writer objects.
Concurrent edits to different fields merge and both author/time/changed-field/value edit events survive.
Forty deterministic seeded delivery orders, including duplicate updates, converge to the same field values, edit list, and Yjs state fingerprint.
Concurrent addition of different heuristics converges in another forty seeded delivery orders.
Deleting a heuristic concurrently with a nested edit converges to deletion without resurrection in the committed fixture.
Invalid IDs fail before collection mutation; identical local edit replay is idempotent and conflicting local reuse fails before mutation.

Disconnected peers store edit events under peer-specific internal keys; if they independently reuse one public edit ID, merged projection detects the duplicate and fails closed instead of silently choosing one event.

Reads reject malformed schema, identity, fields, timestamps, priorities, or incomplete edit history rather than projecting them as valid heuristics, and malformed edit-field containers fail closed without throwing.
Read projections detach edit fields and change objects, so callers cannot mutate shared Yjs state through returned plugin-facing values.
P-04 is recorded as implemented_unverified with source, tests, and this evidence file; no UI-complete claim is made.
notProved: A visible heuristics block/editor, keyboard or pointer interaction, accessibility, or theme behavior.
Positive/negative examples, votes, notes, context labels, scenario links, or an explainable heuristic evaluator.
Two-machine remote collaboration, awareness/presence, authentication, authorization, or identity verification.
Heuristics can invoke a local or remote model, mutate policy text, or run through the plugin host.
Semantic correctness or research usefulness of a heuristic.
